projectName: Medupi and Kusile Power Stations — Final Commissioning
description: Completion of the 4,764 MW Medupi and 4,800 MW Kusile coal-fired power stations. Both stations are the largest dry-cooled power stations in the world. Final units at both stations being commissioned.
risks: Technical defects on boiler welds. Cost overruns. Environmental compliance.
notes: Medupi Unit 1 final commissioning completed. Kusile Units 5 and 6 expected Q3 2025. Total project cost significantly exceeded original budget.

description: Risk Mitigation Independent Power Producer Procurement Programme procuring 2,000 MW of emergency power from IPPs including gas, solar, and wind. Eskom as off-taker.
risks: Grid connection delays. Gas supply constraints. Regulatory approvals.
notes: 1,200 MW of RMIPPPP capacity now operational. Remaining 800 MW expected by end 2025.

description: Procurement of 1,064 locomotives from CRRC (China), CNR (China), and Bombardier (Canada) to modernise Transnet Freight Rail's locomotive fleet. Subject to SIU investigation into procurement irregularities.
risks: SIU investigation outcomes. Locomotive technical defects. Delivery delays.
notes: 764 locomotives delivered. 300 remaining on order. SIU investigation ongoing — no criminal charges yet.

companyName: South African National Roads Agency SOC Limited (SANRAL)
description: Construction of 112 km of new national road through the Eastern Cape Wild Coast, including the Msikaba and Mtentu bridges — the two longest suspension bridges in Africa.
risks: Environmental authorisation delays. Community land claims. Contractor performance.
notes: Msikaba Bridge 85% complete. Mtentu Bridge 60% complete. Environmental authorisation for final section expected Q3 2025.

companyName: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa (PRASA)
description: Procurement of 3,600 new passenger rail coaches from Gibela Rail Transport Consortium (Alstom/Ubumbano) to replace PRASA's aging fleet. Coaches manufactured at Dunnottar factory in Ekurhuleni.
risks: Vandalism of new coaches. Delivery delays. Infrastructure readiness.
notes: 1,368 coaches delivered. Manufacturing at Dunnottar progressing. Vandalism of new coaches remains a significant challenge.
